rows.title: The RTI shall implement object-management services for registration, update, delete, discovery, and interaction delivery behavior

rows.title: Joined federates shall use DM services to declare intent to generate information.
A joined federate shall invoke appropriate DM services before registering objects, updating attributes, or sending interactions.
Joined federates shall use DM or DDM services before discovering objects, reflecting attributes, or receiving interactions.
DM effects shall be independent of federate logical time.
Each FDD class shall have at most one immediate superclass.
Object classes shall expose declared and inherited available attributes.
Interaction classes shall expose declared and inherited available parameters.
RTI shall track published object-class attributes per joined federate.
RTI shall track subscribed object-class attributes per joined federate.
RTI shall track published interaction classes per joined federate.
RTI shall track subscribed interaction classes per joined federate.
RTI shall support interaction between DM subscriptions and DDM subscriptions.

rows.notes: This canonical narrowed row captures the repo-owned subscription-side update-rate behavior: explicit and FOM-declared designators propagate across direct, inherited, and regioned subscriptions within the logical-time model.
rows.title: RTI shall apply explicit and FOM-declared update-rate designators across direct, inherited, and region-based object-class subscriptions within the implemented logical-time subset.
RTI shall provide Publish Object Class Attributes.
Publish Object Class Attributes shall declare attributes a federate may update for instances of an object class.
RTI shall reject publication requests for unavailable attributes.
RTI shall provide Unpublish Object Class Attributes.
Unpublishing shall remove the federate’s ability to update the specified attributes.
RTI shall provide Publish Interaction Class.
Publication of an interaction class shall permit the federate to send interactions of that class.
RTI shall provide Unpublish Interaction Class.
Unpublishing an interaction class shall remove the federate’s ability to send interactions of that class.
RTI shall provide Subscribe Object Class Attributes.
Subscribing to object-class attributes shall make matching object instances discoverable when discovery conditions are met.
Subscribing to object-class attributes shall make matching in-scope attribute updates reflectable.
RTI shall provide Unsubscribe Object Class Attributes.
Unsubscribing shall remove the federate’s interest in the specified object-class attributes.
RTI shall provide Subscribe Interaction Class.
Subscribing to an interaction class shall make matching interactions receivable.
RTI shall provide Unsubscribe Interaction Class.
RTI shall invoke Start Registration For Object Class when registration becomes useful for subscribed federates.
RTI shall invoke Stop Registration For Object Class when registration is no longer useful for subscribed federates.
RTI shall invoke Turn Interactions On when a published interaction class has matching subscribers.
RTI shall invoke Turn Interactions Off when a published interaction class no longer has matching subscribers.
RTI shall support registration, modification, and deletion of object instances.
